entries.definition:
borrowing from Syriac. Of this root, only عَدْن ʿadn, occurs 11 times in the Qur’an.
عَدْن ʿadn [v. n./n.] everlasting, permanent, settling in (40:8) وَأَدْخِلْهُمْ جَنَّـٰتِ عَدْنٍ ٱلَّتِى وَعَدتَّهُمْ
[and] admit them to the Garden of everlasting abode You have promised them.
entries.definition_english:
borrowing from Syriac. Of this root, only عَدْن ʿadn, occurs 11 times in the Qur’an.
عَدْن ʿadn [v. n./n.] everlasting, permanent, settling in (40:8) وَأَدْخِلْهُمْ جَنَّـٰتِ عَدْنٍ ٱلَّتِى وَعَدتَّهُمْ
[and] admit them to the Garden of everlasting abode You have promised them.

entries.definition:
ʿ–d–w/y the two sides of a valley, to cross from one side to the other, to run, to pass, to infect, infection, calamity; aggression, animosity, enemy, corruption; to boycott. Of this root, 15 forms occur 105 times in the Qur’an: تَعْدُوا۟ taʿdū three times; عَادَىٰ ʿādā once; يَتَعَدَّىٰ yataʿaddā three times; ٱعْتَدَىٰ iʿtadā 15 times; عَدْو ʿadw twice; عَادٍ ʿādī three times; عَادُون ʿādūn three times; عَادِيَات ʿādiyāt once; مُعْتَدٍ muʿtadī three times; مُعْتَدُون muʿtadūn six times; عَدُوّ ʿaduww 42 times; أَعْدَاء ʾaʿdāʾ seven times; عَدَاوَة ʿadāwatun six times; عُدْوَان ʿudwān eight times and عُدْوَة ʿudwatun twice.
تَعْدُوا۟ taʿdū [imperf. of v. intrans.] 1 to violate the commands, to transgress (4:154) لَا تَعْدُوا۟ فِى ٱلسَّبْتِ
do not violate the Sabbath 2 to turn away from, to abandon (18:28) وَلَا تَعْدُ عَيْنَاكَ عَنْهُمْ
and your eyes should not turn away from them.

عَادَىٰ ʿādā [v. III, trans.] to feud with, to treat or take, as an enemy, to exchange hostility with (60:7) عَسَى ٱللَّهُ أَن يَجْعَلَ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَ ٱلَّذِينَ عَادَيْتُم مِّنْهُم مَّوَدَّةً
God may well bring about affection between you and those of them you take to be an enemy.

يَتَعَدَّىٰ yataʿaddā [imperf. of v. V, trans.] to transgress, to overstep the limits (4:14) وَمَن يَعْصِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَيَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَهُۥ
but whoever disobeys God and His Messenger and oversteps His limits.

ٱعْتَدَىٰ iʿtadā [v. VIII, intrans.] 1 to assault, to commit an aggression (2:194) فَمَنِ ٱعْتَدَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فَٱعْتَدُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ بِمِثْلِ مَا ٱعْتَدَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ
so whoever commits aggression against you, attack him as he attacked you 2 to violate the commands, to act unlawfully (2:65) وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْتُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱعْتَدَوْا۟ مِنكُمْ فِى ٱلسَّبْتِ
you know about those of you who violated the Sabbath 3 to commit perjury (5:107) فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِٱللَّهِ لَشَهَـٰدَتُنَآ أَحَقُّ مِن شَهَـٰدَتِهِمَا وَمَا ٱعْتَدَيْنَا
let them swear by God, ‘Our testimony is

607
entries.definition_english:
ʿ–d–w/y the two sides of a valley, to cross from one side to the other, to run, to pass, to infect, infection, calamity; aggression, animosity, enemy, corruption; to boycott. Of this root, 15 forms occur 105 times in the Qur’an: تَعْدُوا۟ taʿdū three times; عَادَىٰ ʿādā once; يَتَعَدَّىٰ yataʿaddā three times; ٱعْتَدَىٰ iʿtadā 15 times; عَدْو ʿadw twice; عَادٍ ʿādī three times; عَادُون ʿādūn three times; عَادِيَات ʿādiyāt once; مُعْتَدٍ muʿtadī three times; مُعْتَدُون muʿtadūn six times; عَدُوّ ʿaduww 42 times; أَعْدَاء ʾaʿdāʾ seven times; عَدَاوَة ʿadāwatun six times; عُدْوَان ʿudwān eight times and عُدْوَة ʿudwatun twice.

but whoever disobeys God and His Messenger and oversteps His limits.

ٱعْتَدَىٰ iʿtadā [v. VIII, intrans.] 1 to assault, to commit an aggression (2:194) فَمَنِ ٱعْتَدَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فَٱعْتَدُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ بِمِثْلِ مَا ٱعْتَدَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ
so whoever commits aggression against you, attack him as he attacked you 2 to violate the commands, to act unlawfully (2:65) وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْتُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱعْتَدَوْا۟ مِنكُمْ فِى ٱلسَّبْتِ
you know about those of you who violated the Sabbath 3 to commit perjury (5:107) فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِٱللَّهِ لَشَهَـٰدَتُنَآ أَحَقُّ مِن شَهَـٰدَتِهِمَا وَمَا ٱعْتَدَيْنَا
let them swear by God, ‘Our testimony is

607

entries.definition:
more worthy [of being accepted] than theirs and we have not [transgressed] perjured ourselves.’
عَدْو ʿadw [v. n./n.] aggressing (10:90) فَأَتْبَعَهُمْ فِرْعَوْنُ وَجُنُودُهُۥ بَغْيًا وَعَدْوًا
but Pharaoh and his troops pursued them in aggression and tyranny.

عَادٍ ʿādī [act. part. pl. عَادُون ʿādūn, fem. pl. عَادِيَات ʿādiyāt] 1 one who commits an aggression or violation (2:173) فَمَنِ ٱضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ بَاغٍ وَلَا عَادٍ
but whoever is forced [to eat such things] by necessity, rather than out of desire to transgress or violate the norms 2 those who charge, attack, raid (100:1) وَٱلْعَـٰدِيَـٰتِ ضَبْحًا
by the charging steeds panting [in their speed]; * ٱلْعَـٰدِيَـٰت name of Sura 100, Meccan sura, so-named because of the reference in verse 1 to ‘the Chargers’.

مُعْتَدٍ muʿtadī [act. part., pl. مُعْتَدُون muʿtadūn] aggressor, assailant, violator of the commands (50:25) مَّنَّاعٍ لِّلْخَيْرِ مُعْتَدٍ مُّرِيبٍ
hinderer of the good, aggressor and a dubious/doubting character.

عَدُوّ ʿaduww I [n./quasi-act. part., pl. أَعْدَاء ʾaʿdāʾ] an enemy (35:6) إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ
indeed, Satan is an enemy to you II [coll. n.] 1 the enemy, enemies (18:50) أَفَتَتَّخِذُونَهُۥ وَذُرِّيَّتَهُۥٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ مِن دُونِى وَهُمْ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ
even though they are enemies of yours? 2 [adjectivally] in a state of animosity, hostile (4:92) مِّن قَوْمٍ عَدُوٍّ لَّكُمْ
from a people hostile to you.

عَدَاوَة ʿadāwatun [v. n./n.] animosity, enmity, hostility (5:14) فَأَغْرَيْنَا بَيْنَهُمُ ٱلْعَدَاوَةَ وَٱلْبَغْضَآءَ
so We stirred up enmity and hatred among them.

عُدْوَان ʿudwān [n./v. n.] 1 aggression, transgression (5:2) وَلَا تَعَاوَنُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلْإِثْمِ وَٱلْعُدْوَانِ
and do not help one another on sin and aggression 2 blame, sanction, censure (28:28) أَيَّمَا ٱلْأَجَلَيْنِ قَضَيْتُ فَلَا عُدْوَانَ عَلَىَّ
whichever of the two terms I fulfil, let there be no reproach against me.

عُدْوَة ʿudwatun [n.] side of the valley (8:42) إِذْ أَنتُم بِٱلْعُدْوَةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُم بِٱلْعُدْوَةِ ٱلْقُصْوَىٰ
remember when you were on the near side [of the valley], and they were on the far side.

608
entries.definition_english:
more worthy [of being accepted] than theirs and we have not [transgressed] perjured ourselves.’

608
